title: Variables & Value Types
description: Understand uint, int, bool, address, bytes, and default values in Solidity.

concept.definition: Solidity contracts use strongly typed state and local variables, with explicit value-size types like uint256.
concept.analogy: State variables are permanent ledger fields; local variables are temporary scratch notes.
keyPoints: uint256 is the common integer type
Public state vars auto-generate getter functions

title: Functions & Visibility
description: Learn function declarations, visibility (public/private/internal/external), and return values.

concept.definition: Functions define contract behavior and can be restricted by visibility and mutability modifiers.
concept.analogy: Visibility is door access: public is open lobby, private is back office.

title: Control Flow
description: Use if/else, require, and conditional checks in smart contract logic.

concept.definition: Conditional logic in Solidity often combines if/else with guard functions like require().
concept.analogy: require is a gatekeeper that rejects invalid transactions at the door.
keyPoints: revert stops and rolls back state changes
Custom errors reduce gas vs long revert strings

title: Loops & Gas Awareness
description: Use for/while carefully and understand gas costs of iteration.

concept.definition: Loops are supported, but unbounded iteration can cause gas-limit failures.
concept.analogy: Each loop step costs fuel; long loops can run out mid-transaction.
keyPoints: Use events/off-chain indexing for heavy scans
Use memory arrays for temporary processing

title: Arrays & Mappings
description: Store grouped data with dynamic arrays and key-value mappings.

concept.definition: Arrays preserve order while mappings provide efficient key-based lookup.
concept.analogy: Arrays are lists; mappings are hash-based address books.
keyPoints: Mapping keys are not enumerable on-chain
Dynamic arrays can grow with push
Reading missing mapping key returns default value

title: Structs & Enums
description: Model rich contract state with structured records and finite status values.

concept.definition: Structs group related fields and enums represent finite state transitions.
concept.analogy: Structs are form records; enums are controlled status labels.
keyPoints: Structs can be stored in mappings and arrays
Use enum for workflow stage safety
Default enum value is first member

title: Modifiers
description: Reuse access and validation logic with function modifiers.

concept.definition: Modifiers wrap function execution with pre/post checks using _ placeholder.
concept.analogy: A modifier is a reusable security checkpoint before function body execution.
keyPoints: Keep modifier logic simple and auditable

title: Inheritance & Interfaces
description: Build composable contracts with inheritance, abstract contracts, and interfaces.

concept.definition: Solidity supports multiple inheritance and interface-driven design for modular contracts.
concept.analogy: Interfaces are promises; inheritance is extension wiring.
keyPoints: Abstract contracts can include implemented logic
Use override with explicit base list when needed

title: Events & Logging
description: Emit events for off-chain indexing, monitoring, and frontend updates.

concept.definition: Events write logs to the transaction receipt for off-chain consumers.
concept.analogy: Events are blockchain receipts your app can subscribe to.
keyPoints: Use indexed fields for searchable topics
Events are not readable on-chain like state
Events are cheaper than storing full history on-chain

title: Error Handling
description: Use require, revert, assert, and custom errors safely.

concept.definition: Solidity error primitives revert transaction state and can return error data.
concept.analogy: Errors are rollback triggers that cancel invalid state changes.

title: Payable & Ether Transfer
description: Receive ETH with payable functions and transfer value safely.

concept.definition: Payable functions accept Ether and can update accounting state based on msg.value.
concept.analogy: Payable is the contract cashier window for ETH.
keyPoints: Prefer call for flexible gas forwarding

title: Security Basics
description: Learn reentrancy prevention, access control, and safe contract patterns.

concept.definition: Secure Solidity design minimizes attack surface through disciplined state flow and authorization checks.
concept.analogy: Smart contract security is vault design: layered controls and minimal trust assumptions.
keyPoints: Use reentrancy guards for external calls
